name: web-auth
description: Authentication patterns for React web applications. Use when implementing login flows, OAuth, JWT handling, session management, or protected routes in React web apps.
capabilities: Implement cookie-based authentication with httpOnly cookies
Build OAuth login flows with Google Identity Services
Create protected routes in React Router and Next.js
Handle automatic token refresh and session management
Add CSRF protection and security headers
Set up NextAuth.js with credentials and OAuth providers
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing legitimate authentication patterns. All 69 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner misidentified documentation text (httpOnly cookie attributes, localStorage references, fetch API examples, environment variable patterns) as security issues. No executable code, scripts, or malicious operations exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Building secure login flows and session management in React requires understanding multiple authentication patterns. This skill provides production-ready code examples for JWT, OAuth, cookies, and protected routes.
limitations: Backend server code is not provided (FastAPI examples are reference only)
No database integration or user storage patterns

useCases.title: Build login pages
useCases.description: Create secure login forms with validation, error handling, and redirect logic after authentication.

useCases.title: Connect React to auth backends
useCases.description: Integrate frontend authentication with backend session management using cookies and JWT tokens.

useCases.title: Harden authentication flows
useCases.description: Add CSRF protection, security headers, and proper token storage patterns to prevent common vulnerabilities.

promptTemplates.title: Basic login setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement a login page with email and password authentication in React using the Auth Context pattern from the web-auth skill.

promptTemplates.title: Google OAuth
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I implement Google OAuth login in a React application using Google Identity Services? Include the button setup and token handling.

promptTemplates.title: Protected routes
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a protected route component that redirects unauthenticated users to login while preserving the intended destination URL.

promptTemplates.title: Token refresh
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the token refresh pattern for automatically renewing expired JWT tokens without forcing users to log in again.

bestPractices: Use httpOnly, secure cookies instead of localStorage for tokens to prevent XSS theft
Implement automatic token refresh to handle expired sessions gracefully
Add CSRF protection when using cookies with credentials: 'include'
antiPatterns: Storing JWT tokens in localStorage - vulnerable to XSS attacks
Storing sensitive data in browser storage without encryption
Not validating tokens on the server before trusting authenticated requests

faq.question: What is the most secure way to store auth tokens in React?
faq.answer: httpOnly cookies are the most secure. They cannot be accessed by JavaScript, protecting against XSS attacks.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Next.js authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes NextAuth.js configuration examples and middleware patterns for protecting routes in the Next.js App Router.

faq.question: Does this skill include backend authentication code?
faq.answer: Backend code is reference only. The FastAPI examples show how to set cookies and validate tokens but are not complete implementations.

faq.question: Is the authentication code safe to use as-is?
faq.answer: The patterns follow security best practices. Always audit and test in your specific environment before production use.

faq.question: What OAuth providers are covered?
faq.answer: The skill includes Google OAuth examples and NextAuth.js configuration that supports Google, GitHub, and credentials-based login.

faq.question: How does this compare to other authentication libraries?
faq.answer: This skill teaches core patterns. For production apps, consider NextAuth.js or Auth0 which handle edge cases and security updates automatically.
